content:
2 min read
2 hours ago

--

But real-world products like Amazon don’t use them separately…
They combine them intelligently.
Let’s break down the Amazon homepage like a system designer 👇
---
🛒 Imagine you open Amazon homepage
Different sections behave differently:
---
🔹 1. Personalized Header (SSR)
“Hello Abubakar”
“Your Orders”
“Recommended for you”
👉 This data is user-specific and changes on every request.
So Amazon uses Server-Side Rendering (SSR)
Example:
When you hit the homepage → server fetches:
- user info
- browsing history
- recommendations
Then sends ready HTML.
💡 Why SSR?
Because personalization must be accurate on first load.
---
🔹 2. Hero Banner & Categories (SSG)
Top banner:
“Great Indian Festival Sale”
Static category blocks
👉 Same for every user
So this is pre-built at build time using Static Site Generation (SSG)
Example:
- Banner changes once per day
- Categories rarely change
💡 Why SSG?
Fastest load + best SEO
---
🔹 3. Today’s Deals (ISR)
Deals section changes frequently but not every second.
👉 Amazon uses Incremental Static Regeneration (ISR)
Example:
- Page generated once
- Cached
- Auto refresh every few minutes
revalidate: 60
💡 Why ISR?
Balance between performance & freshness
---
🔹 4. Recommendations & Interactions (CSR)
जब आप scroll करते हो:
- New products load होते हैं
- Filters apply होते हैं
- Cart update होता है
👉 This is Client-Side Rendering (CSR)
Example:
React fetch करता है:
useEffect(() => {
fetch("/api/recommendations")
}, [])
💡 Why CSR?
Because it's interactive & real-time
---
🔥 Final Architecture (Real Learning)
Amazon homepage is NOT:
❌ SSR app
❌ CSR app
It is a HYBRID SYSTEM:
✔ SSR → personalization
✔ SSG → static sections
✔ ISR → semi-dynamic content
✔ CSR → interactions
---
🚀 Developer Mindset Shift
Stop thinking:
“Which is better? SSR or CSR?”
Start thinking:
“Which part of my UI needs what?”
That’s how real-world scalable apps are built.
---
💬 If you're preparing for 7–15 LPA roles,
this is the level of thinking interviewers expect.
Not just hooks…
but architecture.
#ReactJS #NextJS #FrontendDeveloper #SystemDesign #WebPerformance #SoftwareEngineering
